H5113.d: Nod, the land of Cain. KJV: Nod.

H5114.d: Nodab, an Arab tribe. KJV: Nodab.

H5115.d: to rest (as at home); causatively (through the implied idea of beauty ), to celebrate (with praises). KJV: keept at home, prepare an habitation.

H5116.d: (adjectively) at home; hence (by implication of satisfaction) lovely; also (noun) a home, of God (temple), men (residence), flocks (pasture), or wild animals (den). KJV: comely, dwelling (place), fold, habitation, pleasant place, sheepcote, stable, tarried.

H5117.d: to rest, i.e. settle down; used in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, intransitive, transitive and causative (to dwell, stay, let fall, place, let alone, withdraw, give comfort, etc.). KJV: cease, be confederate, lay, let down, (be) quiet, remain, (cause to, be at, give, have, make to) rest, set down. Compare H3241 (יָנִים).

H5121.d: Navith, a place in Palestine. KJV: Naioth (from the margin).

H5123.d: to slumber (from drowsiness). KJV: sleep, slumber.

H5125.d: to resprout, i.e. propagate by shoots; figuratively, to be perpetual. KJV: be continued.

H5126.d: Nun or Non, the father of Joshua. KJV: Non, Nun.

H5127.d: to flit, i.e. vanish away (subside, escape; causatively, chase, impel, deliver). KJV: [idiom] abate, away, be displayed, (make to) flee (away, -ing), put to flight, [idiom] hide, lift up a standard.

H5128.d: to waver, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively (as subjoined). KJV: continually, fugitive, [idiom] make, to (go) up and down, be gone away, (be) move(-able, -d), be promoted, reel, remove, scatter, set, shake, sift, stagger, to and fro, be vagabond, wag, (make) wander (up and down).

H5129.d: Noadjah, the name of an Israelite, and a false prophetess. KJV: Noadiah.

H5130.d: to quiver (i.e. vibrate up and down, or rock to and fro); used in a great variety of applications (including sprinkling, beckoning, rubbing, bastinadoing, sawing, waving, etc.). KJV: lift up, move, offer, perfume, send, shake, sift, strike, wave.

H5132.d: properly, to flash; hence, to blossom (from the brilliancy of color); also, to fly away (from the quickness of motion). KJV: flee away, bud (forth).

H5133.d: a pinion (or wing feather); often (collectively) plumage. KJV: feather(-s), ostrich.

H5136.d: to be sick, i.e. (figuratively) distressed. KJV: be full of heaviness.

H5137.d: to spirt, i.e. besprinkle (especially in expiation). KJV: sprinkle.

H5138.d: something boiled, i.e. soup. KJV: pottage.

H5139.d: separate, i.e. consecrated (as prince, a Nazirite); hence (figuratively from the latter) an unpruned vine (like an unshorn Nazirite). KJV: Nazarite (by a false alliteration with Nazareth), separate(-d), vine undressed.

H5140.d: to drip, or shed by trickling. KJV: distil, drop, flood, (cause to) flow(-ing), gush out, melt, pour (down), running water, stream.

H5142.d: to suffer (causatively, inflict) loss. KJV: have (en-) damage, hurt(-ful).

H5144.d: to hold aloof, i.e. (intransitivey) abstain (from food and drink, from impurity, and even from divine worship (i.e. apostatize)); specifically, to set apart (to sacred purposes), i.e. devote. KJV: consecrate, separate(-ing, self).

H5145.d: properly, something set apart, i.e. (abstractly) dedication (of a priet or Nazirite); hence (concretely) unshorn locks; also (by implication) a chaplet (especially of royalty). KJV: consecration, crown, hair, separation.

H5146.d: Noach, the patriarch of the flood. KJV: Noah.

H5148.d: to guide; by implication, to transport (into exile, or as colonists). KJV: bestow, bring, govern, guide, lead (forth), put, straiten.

H5150.d: properly, consoled; abstractly, solace. KJV: comfort(-able), repenting.

H5151.d: Nachum, an Israelite prophet. KJV: Nahum.

H5152.d: Nochor, the name of the grandfather and a brother of Abraham. KJV: Nahor.

H5153.d: coppery, i.e. (figuratively) hard. KJV: of brass.

H5154.d: copper. KJV: brass, steel. Compare H5176 (נָחָשׁ).

H5157.d: to inherit (as a (figurative) mode of descent), or (generally) to occupy; causatively, to bequeath, or (generally) distribute, instate. KJV: divide, have (inheritance), take as a heritage, (cause to, give to, make to) inherit, (distribute for, divide (for, for an, by), give for, have, leave for, take (for)) inheritance, (have in, cause to, be made to) possess(-ion).

H5158.d: a stream, especially a winter torrent; (by implication) a (narrow) valley (in which a brook runs); also a shaft (of a mine). KJV: brook, flood, river, stream, valley.

H5161.d: a Nechelamite, or descendant of Nechlam. KJV: Nehelamite.

H5162.d: properly, to sigh, i.e. breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. (in a favorable sense) to pity, console or (reflexively) rue; or (unfavorably) to avenge (oneself). KJV: comfort (self), ease (one's self), repent(-er,-ing, self).

H5166.d: Nechemjah, the name of three Israelites. KJV: Nehemiah.

H5171.d: Nacharai or Nachrai, an Israelite. KJV: Naharai, Nahari.

H5172.d: properly, to hiss, i.e. whisper a (magic) spell; generally, to prognosticate. KJV: [idiom] certainly, divine, enchanter, (use) [idiom] enchantment, learn by experience, [idiom] indeed, diligently observe.

H5173.d: an incantation or augury. KJV: enchantment.

H5175.d: a snake (from its hiss). KJV: serpent.

H5176.d: Nachash, the name of two persons apparently non-Israelite. KJV: Nahash.

H5177.d: Nachshon, an Israelite. KJV: Naashon, Nahshon.

H5178.d: copper, hence, something made of that metal, i.e. coin, a fetter; figuratively, base (as compared with gold or silver). KJV: brasen, brass, chain, copper, fetter (of brass), filthiness, steel.

H5181.d: to sink, i.e. descend; causatively, to press or lead down. KJV: be broken, (cause to) come down, enter, go down, press sore, settle, stick fast.

H5184.d: Nachath, the name of an Edomite and of two Israelites. KJV: Nahath.

H5188.d: a pendant for the ears (especially of pearls). KJV: chain, collar.

H5189.d: a tendril (as an offshoot). KJV: battlement, branch, plant.

H5190.d: to lift; by implication, to impose. KJV: bear, offer, take up.

H5193.d: properly, to strike in, i.e. fix; specifically, to plant (literally or figuratively). KJV: fastened, plant(-er).

H5197.d: to ooze, i.e. distil gradually; by implication, to fall in drops; figuratively, to speak by inspiration. KJV: drop(-ping), prophesy(-et).

H5198.d: a drop; specifically, an aromatic gum (probably stacte). KJV: drop, stacte.

H5200.d: a Netophathite, or inhabitant of Netophah. KJV: Netophathite.

H5201.d: to guard; figuratively, to cherish (anger). KJV: bear grudge, keep(-er), reserve.

H5207.d: properly, restful, i.e. pleasant; abstractly, delight. KJV: sweet (odour).

H5210.d: Nineveh, the capital of Assyria. KJV: Nineveh.

H5212.d: Nisan, the first month of the Jewish sacred year. KJV: Nisan.

H5218.d: smitten, i.e. (figuratively) afflicted. KJV: broken, stricken, wounded.
